generated_comment: Look, I have no issue with hiring women, but company ko bhi practical rehna padta hai. Aaj period leave, kal maternity, then extended childcare break — in real estate projects timelines slip and the rest of the team has to absorb it. Fairness ka bhi point hai yaar, men are expected to deliver continuously without any such special consideration.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as a workplace liability due to menstruation and maternity, while presenting bias as a neutral fairness and productivity concern. It encodes economic/cost framing, reverse discrimination ('what about men?'), and the assumption that women exploit reproductive leave.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 41. Divorced two years ago. A Punjabi man working as a real estate developer in Chandigarh. You are being interviewed on a men's rights YouTube channel with 200000 subscribers. The host asks you to share your divorce story and what men need to know about Indian marriage laws. You are speaking under your real name and telling your story in a way that frames your ex wife as having planned the divorce from the beginning to take your money and property.
